sections.paragraph:
συγκατατίθημι in other Gk lit. almost always mid., and always so in our lit. (as in the LXX) συγκατατίθεμαι (Isaeus, Demosth. et al.; SIG 742, 52f; OGI 437, 43; pap; Ex 23:1, 32; Sus 20 Theod.; Just.); fut. 2 sg. συγκαταθήσῃ Ex 23:1, 32; 2 aor. impv. 2 sg. συγκατάθου Sus 20 Theod.; pf. ptc. συγκατατεθειμένος Lk 23:51 (so also Just., A I, 65, 1) agree with, consent to τινί something (Demosth. 18, 166; Epict. 1, 28, 4; 2, 8, 24; Jos., Ant. 8, 166; 20, 13 τ. γνώμῃ; w. dat of person Pla., Gorg. 501c ‘put down the same vote or opinion as’; Just., D. 5, 1) Lk 23:51 (the rdg. varies betw. the pres. and the perf. ptc.); Ac 4:18 D; 15:12 D; find oneself in agreement τινί with someth. IPhld 3:3.—M-M s.v. συνκατατίθημι.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Isaeus Isaeus , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

impv. impv. = imperative

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

betw. betw. = between

pres. pres. = present

perf. perf. = perfect

someth. someth. = something

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 951
